questions.question: What is the primary role of the Virtual File System (VFS) layer in a kernel?
answers: To provide an abstract uniform interface for applications to access diverse file systems
To manage virtual memory swap spaces on physical storage disks
To buffer incoming network package flows before delivery to sockets
To simulate internal hard drive arrays inside local ram spaces

questions.question: What is a major advantage of a journaling file system like ext4?
answers: It compresses text files to half their initial disk sector allocations
It encrypts all user credentials automatically
It records changes in a log to prevent corruption during sudden power losses
It speeds up graphics card rendering rates

questions.question: What is the main difference between internal and external fragmentation?
answers: Internal is fixed by defragmentation; external requires virtual memory flushes
External happens on hard drives; internal happens inside CPU registers
External happens inside kernel space; internal happens in user space
Internal occurs inside fixed allocated blocks; external occurs between variable blocks

questions.question: What is the role of a Translation Lookaside Buffer (TLB)?
answers: To act as a specialized cache for virtual-to-physical address translations
To store execution metrics logs for background tasks
To map file allocation records sequentially on disk
To handle network data package buffering streams

questions.question: What state does a system enter when it spends more time swapping pages than executing instructions?
answers: Thrashing
Starvation
Paging
Fragmenting

questions.question: What is a page fault?
answers: A physical hardware failure inside RAM modules
An interrupt triggered when a program accesses a page not currently mapped in RAM
A crash caused by syntax compile errors
A data anomaly where two files share the same sector

questions.question: What privilege layer does the operating system kernel run in on modern CPUs?
answers: Hypervisor mode
User mode (Ring 3)
Kernel mode (Ring 0)
Safe mode

questions.question: What is a major advantage of a microkernel compared to a monolithic design?
answers: Smaller total storage size footprint
Faster execution performance speeds
Higher system stability and isolation of corrupted drivers
Lower context switching overhead costs

questions.question: What characterizes a monolithic kernel architecture?
answers: OS services are moved to separate user-space modules
It runs exclusively on virtual machines
It eliminates context switching overhead entirely
All operating system services run inside a single privileged kernel space

questions.question: What happens during a hardware interrupt?
answers: The operating system completely reboots its kernel
The CPU suspends its execution stream to run an interrupt service routine
Virtual memory maps flush physical hard drive tracks
The active process is terminated and marked as a zombie
